messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Landscape Genomics course in Lausanne, EPL, Switzerland
messages.subject_clean: Landscape Genomics course in Lausanne, EPL, Switzerland
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
Mark your calendars! Our Landscape Genomics course will be in person in the beautiful Lausanne, 
EPL, Switzerland on June 17-21! Don't miss out on this incredible opportunity to learn and immerse yourself in a stunning environment: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course17/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course17/ )

Should you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Best regards,
Carlo
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Online course: course on Genome Assembly and Annotation (3 seats left)
messages.subject_clean: Online course: course on Genome Assembly and Annotation (3 seats left)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
I wanted to bring to your attention our upcoming course on Genome Assembly and Annotation, available at: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course20/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course20/ ) (only 3 remaining seats available)
 
Dates: Online, March 18th-22nd
 
This course offers a unique opportunity for biologists and bioinformaticians to delve into the intricacies of de novo genome assembly and annotation. Through a combination of theoretical discussions and practical examples, participants will gain valuable insights into generating high-quality annotated and curated reference genomes.
 
Designed for researchers at all levels of expertise, from beginners to advanced users, the course covers fundamental concepts, algorithms, and workflows. We will explore various aspects of genome assembly and annotation, drawing from the Vertebrate Genomes Project assembly pipeline as a model. Hands-on exercises, utilizing both graphical interfaces and basic command line tools, will reinforce learning and skill development.
 
 
 
For more information on this and other courses and workshops we offer, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course20/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course20/ ) 
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Online course; Multivariate Data Analysis with R and vegan
messages.subject_clean: Online course; Multivariate Data Analysis with R and vegan
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
join us in May (27th-31st) if you want to learn how to perform multivariate data analysis with R and vegan.
 
In this 5-day course, we will prioritise exploring ordination methods and using restricted permutations to analyse various experimental designs. Specifically, we'll delve into the application of multivariate techniques, including unconstrained and constrained ordination (CCA, RDA, Constrained PCoA), alongside between-group tests like PERMANOVA. Topics covered will include design- and model-based permutations, as well as understanding sample exchangeability in tests. Additionally, we'll explore advanced applications of vegan, extending beyond fitting constrained ordination models to include diagnostics, plotting, and more.
 
For more information, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/vegan/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/vegan/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] Online course: Introduction to GWAS
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
We are happy to announce our upcoming course on "Introduction to Genome-Wide Association Studies (GWAS)"! If you're looking to enhance your skills and knowledge in GWAS analysis, this course is tailored for you. Whether you are a student, researcher, or professional, this course will provide you with the essential tools to confidently navigate the world of GWAS.
 
**Course Dates:** Online, 24-28 June 


Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course49/ )
 
This comprehensive course will guide you through the entire process of GWAS data analysis, ensuring that you not only understand the individual steps but also learn how to create a reproducible and efficient bioinformatics workflow.
 
Over the span of five days, our course will cover a range of topics through a well-structured module-based approach. Each day will commence with informative lectures, followed by interactive class discussions to solidify key concepts. Subsequently, you will engage in hands-on practical sessions designed to foster your skills. These sessions will include both guided exercises with our experienced instructors and independent tasks to reinforce your learning. After each exercise, you will have the opportunity to interpret and discuss results within a group setting.

 A background in biology, particularly genetics, is recommended. While a basic familiarity with R programming and Unix will be helpful, our course will provide ample support for learners at varying levels of experience.
 
 
For the full list of our our courses and workshops, please have a look at: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846
